segment-text: Screenplay by: Michael McCullers
SQ50 - OPENING FANTASY
OPEN ON -- A FAMILY PHOTOGRAPH OF THE TEMPLETONS
Time is a funny thing. When I was a kid, the days lasted forever...
(in the background) One, two, three...
Land on SEVEN-YEAR-OLD TIM counting against a tree as his parents and BOSS BABY hide in a bush.
Hide, hide, hide!
Quick! Over here!
Four, five, six...
But the years, well they went by so fast.
Tim, no peeking!
Yeah, no peeking!
The camera slowly circles the tree. On the other side of the tree-- TIM IS NOW ALL GROWN-UP.
Seven, eight, nine... nine and a half... ten!
Before you know it, you're all grown up. It even happened to me.
Ready or not, here I come!
FREEZE-- on the goofy face Tim makes as he bursts out to give chase.
My name's Tim, and this-- well, this is the rest of my story.
Tabitha pulls Tim's new baby, TINA, along as they run from Tim and Tim's wife Carol LAUGHING-CAROL Run for your life!
No fair dad, you peeked!
The scene transitions as the Templetons walk to their car.
They call me a stay-at-home dad, which means I hardly get to stay at home. Let's go!
TABITHA
TIM IS IN A RACE CAR WITH HIS FAMILY Racing around a track.
Buckle up Templetons! Ready? Set! Go!
Now, some might say I still have an overactive imagination, but being a dad is the coolest job in the world! A bunch of cool jobs, really.
Their car rockets ahead across the finish line.
Sometimes, I'm a race car driver.
They pull ahead and cross the finish line... at the grocery store.
Woo! Daddio Andretti!
Yay Templetons!
IN THE KITCHEN-Tim dressed as an OLD WESTERN CARD SHARK, dealing cards to Tabitha and Tina.
(Western voice) Well, and then sometimes I'm the best darn cook in the county, and I deal out every meal.
-- which transforms into Tim cooking multiple grilled cheese
Come on pa, hit me!
Tina BURPS.
Woo doggy! And the house wins again!
MONSTER VERSIONS OF THE TEMPLETONS gather around Tim as he operates on a covered figure.
(Monster voice) Or a surgeon, if need be. Spork. Spork! Spatula. Spatula!
CAROL
TABITHA
Give my creature life!
The Templetons watch as a GIANT LAMB-LAMB rises from the operating table.
She's alive!
Tim, no longer in doctor's scrubs, hands Tabitha the newly sewn Lamb-Lamb while making her GROWL. Tabitha ACTS AFRAID.
We have our ups and our downs...
Lamb-Lamb falls apart, and Tabitha tears up.
No, no, look, look! Lamb Lamb's ok! See!
He pulls one of the loose threads, restoring Lamb-Lamb.
But we always stick together. This is my wife Carol-CAROL It's a miracle!
AT AN AWARDS SHOW Carol is dressed in a gown, receiving an award.
She's the breadwinner of the family...
Instead of a statue, Carol is handed a LARGE BAGUETTE.
Thank you! You know what they say, it's all about the dough. And I couldn't have done it without my husband, he really rose to the occasion.
As the audience cheers, Tim, dressed in a tuxedo T-shirt,
It's the yeast I could do! Yeah mama!
She brings home the bacon, too! IN THE KITCHEN
Tim fries the bacon in a pan.
While I keep an eye on our new baby Tina.
In her high chair, Baby Tina spies on Tim with binoculars.
But sometimes... It feels like she's keeping an eye on me.

Tim looks up to see-- Tina quickly going back to being cute. He turns back around, and she scribbles notes in a note pad. AT THE DINNER TABLE Tabitha wears her new school uniform as they celebrate her good news with cake. A banner reads, "You've Been Accepted!" and then, hand-written, "-- To Second Grade!"
And Tabitha, our little secondgrader-- just got accepted into the best school in town. Thank you!
Yay Tabitha!
We're so proud.
We see an adult JIMBO, STACI, and their baby standing in front of a "Re-elect Jimbo for Mayor" poster.
I still keep in touch with the old gang, too. Believe it or not, Jimbo is mayor now.
Jimbo and Staci take a selfie in front of his campaign
Of course his wife Staci is the real brains behind the operation. Yo, Jimbo!
DRIVER
A car speeds past, revealing three familiar TRIPLETS on motorcycles.
Now, the triplets... Busted. Busted. Busted.
TRIPLET 2 TRIPLET 3
They haven't changed much.
DOUBLE DOORS-- LEAD INTO TED'S OFFICE
And as for my baby brother Ted...
Ted is silhouetted against the city skyline. Great suit, great view. He squeezes Senor Squeaky as he handles multiple calls.
Well, he grew up to be the Boss, all right. He's so busy, we hardly see him any more, but he always remembers to send inappropriately lavish gifts on special occasions.
THE TEMPLETON BACKYARD
Tabitha runs over and gives Precious Templeton the pony a big hug.
Oh em gee! Uncle Ted is the best!
Precious stares daggers at Tim, who holds a shovel. CLOSE-UP ON A MUG It reads, "WORLD'S BEST DAD."
Hey, all in all, my life is pretty perfect.
Pull back and see Tim drinking from the mug, lying on a beach. He's in a TROPICAL PARADISE, with Carol and Tina sitting next to him.
But all that was about to change.
Suddenly a VOLCANO ERUPTS IN THE BACKGROUND. Lava spews everywhere.
(to Carol and Tina) Good heavens! I'll save you!
Tim quickly puts Carol and Tina safely in a tree.
(still reading her magazine) Thanks babe!
Tim looks around and spots Tabitha surrounded by books as the lava approaches.
(calling out) Stay calm, Tabitha!
He turns his beach chair into a surfboard and begins surfing the lava.
I'm coming for you! I'm going to
save you from the bubbling lava-Suddenly the lava parts and Tim's surfboard grounds to a halt in front of a very focused, no-nonsense seven-year-old working at a desk.
Dad, what are you doing?!
The rest of the scene dissolves, leaving Tim in the living room holding a roll of Mentos and a soda bottle.
Ew, gross, it went everywhere!
Come on, this is the candy volcano... of doom! You used to love it!
But I'm trying to do my homework... or I'm doomed.
She gathers her stack of books and leaves. Oh...
Because if there's one thing I've learned, it's that you're only a kid once. Once you grow up, you can never go back.
SQ300 - BEDTIME INT. TABITHA'S ROOM - NIGHT

Tabitha flosses diligently in the reflection of an award. She smiles, then looks to the photo of Rosie the Riveter taped to the wall and imitates her pose before going about her night time routine. She lays out her uniform, making sure to give its acorn button a shine when she sees a spot. Oh no!
She feeds her fish.
(to fish) Good night Dr. Hawking!
With her hand over her heart, Tabitha recites the periodic table with all of the reverence of the Pledge of Allegiance.
Carbon, boron, beryllium, lithium, helium, hydrogen. (opens her eyes) Nailed it! Boom shakalaka!
Tabitha climbs under the covers for some light reading before bed: a book of math jokes.
(laughing) Carry the four! Gosh...
Tabitha looks up from the math equations to see her entire family standing over her.
Hey there Tabitha, it's the Good Night Show live from your bedroom, starring dad and mom-CAROL And special guest, baby Tina!
Tabitha gives Tina a big kiss.
Good night little Tina!
(to Tina) Come back here you!
Tabitha kisses Carol's cheek.
Good night mom!
Carol kisses her back.
Good night sweetheart.
Carol exits with Tina as Tim sits down on the edge of the bed with his GUITAR.
(to Tina) Momma's gonna kiss you, momma's gonna kiss you...
He starts to sing-TIM
GOOD NIGHT SONG WITH YOUR DAD...
Tabitha takes out her EARBUDS--
It's time for your goodnight song.
Oh no thanks... (puts back in earbuds) I'm listening to white noise. Helps
me fall asleep.
Hey, what would you say to me helping you rehearse for the holiday pageant?
Tabitha clams up.
Yes! Right? I can come to school with you, and we can get all of your friends together, and we can rehearse together, go get some ice cream after, my treat.
No! You can't do that. Um, liability issues. It's this whole thing...

Oh, right, right. Right. Oh! How about your favorite bedtime story? You know, the one about-TABITHA (loudly) Uncle Ted was a magical talking baby, there was a rocket full of puppies, and you saved the world...
Tim removes her earbuds.
Shhh, it was a good story, wasn't it?
Well, it didn't really make a lot of sense...
The jokes were good, right?
Tabitha gives a noncommittal shrug.
(yawning) Dad, my schedule is brutal tomorrow...
Yeah, my schedule's pretty brutal too. Don't forget Lamb Lamb!
He reaches over to where Lamb Lamb leans against a small shrine to Uncle Ted.
Good night Lamb Lamb!
She kisses Lamb Lamb then turns to the photo.
Good night Uncle Ted! I hope to grow up and be a success just like you! Maybe we'll see you soon.
Good night, Tabitha.
He leans in for a goodnight kiss, but-She sticks out her hand.
Don't you think I'm a little old for that now?
Confused, Tim sticks out his hand and she SHAKES IT. Ok...
I think it's time we both grow up. I look forward to greeting you at the breakfast table!
She puts a sleep mask over her eyes and hugs her book of math jokes to her chest.
Sweet dreams.
Tim, clearly hurt, turns out the lights and closes the door. SQ400 - TIM TIME
IN THE ATTIC - LATER

Among the usual attic stuff are the CONTENTS OF TIM'S OLD ROOM, arranged almost like they were when he was a kid. He pulls out a box of Christmas ornaments and several COLORFUL BEADS fall out. Tim picks one up and flops back on his old bed, reminiscing.
Where has the time gone?
MUFFLED VOICE O.S.
A one-armed figure wearing a T-Rex head struggles out of a BOX.
Wait, wait! I see light! Out of my way! (inhales deeply) At long last! The sweet breath of freedom!
He removes the T-Rex head to reveal-- it's WIZZIE. Wizzie?!
You there! What century is this?
Tim reaches out and grabs Wizzie.
Unhand me vile ogre!
No, no, no, no Wizzie, it's me! It's Tim!
Is it really you?
Wizzie reaches his (only) arm out to Tim.
Come closer... Let me see your face... closer... closer still...
Tim brings Wizzie right up to his face.
Oh Timothy...
Wizzie draws his arm back... and SLAPS Tim.
Hey! What was that for?
You cast me into eternal darkness and wreaked havoc on my Circadian rhythms!
Wizzie shakes his arm angrily and the numbers on his clock flip erratically.
I'm sorry, Wizzie. Hey, but you look great though.
Except for the arm, you mean.
I didn't even notice.
You thought it!
I mean, I noticed. Because you don't have an arm-WIZZIE You would never treat Lamb Lamb this way.
Well I... I gave her to my daughter, Tabitha.
You have produced an heir, Timothy!
Two, actually!
Two?! Let the bells ring! Yes! Yes! It's a time for celebration!
Wizzie shakes his arm excitedly as his ALARM RINGS. Tim quickly turns the off the alarm.
No, no! No, we have to stop! Wizzie, shhh be quiet! No, no, no, not the time for celebration! Wizzie, shh quiet!
What is it Tim?
I don't know, I guess I'm just not feeling very celebratory right now.
Tim places Wizzie on the night stand.
Tabitha... Man, Wizzie, she's growing up so fast. It's like she doesn't even need me anymore.
(commiserating) Yes... first they start spending less time with you. Mmmm.
Then they stop coming to you for advice. Exactly.
Soon they take you for granted... I guess.
Then break your arm-What?
-- and stuff you into a box!
Ok, Wizzie, you're not helping. Wizzie GRUMBLES.
Ok, I'm just... I'm afraid Tabitha and I are growing apart. Like... Like I did with my brother.
(sighs sympathetically) Oh...
I thought we had all the time in the world... But you can't turn back time, can you? Tim's (rhetorical) question hangs in the air. Then-WIZZIE How dare you question my power?!
Wizzie starts windmilling his arm(s) backwards while invoking
Oh, here we go.
Just then, Tim HEARS SOMETHING. He hits Wizzie's snooze button, and Wizzie begins to SNORE.
Wait. Did you hear that?
Wizzie wakes up.
Never abuse the snooze button!
Sh! It sounds like it's coming from the baby's room.
He presses his ear to the floor.
What is it? A creature of the night? Witches? Is it the baying of the hounds of hell? Wizzie!

Tim heads towards the stairs. Wizzie calls out after him-WIZZIE Don't go, Tim! I can turn back time! To the Golden Years, when it was just you and me-In his excitement, he falls off the night stand, onto the bed, and to the floor.
My good arm...
MOMENTS LATER - IN THE HALLWAY Tim walks quietly toward the nursery, filled with deja vu.
(faintly) Don't open the door... remember last time...
From inside, he hears a VOICE. Curious, he-SQ500 - NOT AGAIN PUSHES OPEN THE NURSERY DOOR The nursery is dark. Tim looks at the FISHER PRICE TOY PHONE in Tina's crib... holds it up to his ear. Hello?
If you would like to make a call, please hang up and try again. (MORE)
If you need help, hang up and then dial your operator. Thank you.
Then Tim sees HIS CELL PHONE, lying on the floor-TIM
He turns back to the crib.
Oh Tina, you little klepto-He pulls back the covers, revealing STORY TIME BEAR.
(shudders) Maniac!
Just then-- a SKITTERING SOUND. A shadowy shape runs behind
Whoa!
He turns, sees nothing... except a PAIR OF EYES. Hi daddy. CHILD'S VOICE O.S. Tim draws back.
T- Tabitha? Is that you?
BABY TINA EMERGES from the shadows.
Ha! You should've seen your face! Oh, it's priceless!
Tim FREAKS OUT-TIM You're-- you're-- you-Still laughing, Tina playfully imitates Tim's reaction.
You can talk!
Baby Tina rips off her PJs in one motion to reveal that she's WEARING A SUIT UNDERNEATH.
Yep! I'm in the family business! It's a clip on tie, shhh... You see, daddy, BabyCorp is more of
what I call a side-Tim FAINTS.
BABY TINA (CONT'D)
Tim begins to come to with Tina leaning over him.
Daddy, daddy, come in! Can you hear me? Daddy! Do you read me? Daddy,
how many fingers am I holding up?
I should've given you a warning. I forgot you were an old man!
Tim sputters on the milk she gives him.
Wait, wait. You're from BabyCorp?

Yes! BabyCorp is more of what I call a side hustle for me. The point is, I am all in on the Templetons, go Templetons! You guys really know how to baby a baby, I mean it's incredible! Not that it's all five star, I have to say, you've got a lot of issues. We'll talk about that later.
Oh my gosh, I can't believe this! Hey, wanna go scare the crap outta mom?
Daddy, please stay focused.
Oh! Right, right, right.
Tina begins to bounce around the room, closing the blinds, checking under doors, listening for spies.
There's a crisis at BabyCorp!
What is it this time? Kittens?!
No! This time it's even worse!
Worse than kittens?!
Yes! That's why I volunteered for this super secret assignment.
I want to help! What do you want me to do?
That's the spirit daddy! You're exactly who I need! Yes!
To get Uncle Ted.
Tim makes a face.

What's with the face? Daddy, BabyCorp can't wait any longer. All the pressure's on me, pop. When I volunteered for this gig I thought it would be easy, but you two never see each other. It's so sad. It's like F minus, see me after class.
It hits Tim how far apart he and his brother have grown.
Sad? Yeah, maybe, a little. I don't know...
Did you forget to call him? I noticed you can be a little absentminded.
Sorry, what?
It's just that you tend to procrastinate. You have to get him here for the holidays.
No, I'm not-- No, I'm not gonna call him right now. No.
Daddy, can't never could! (then) Did you two have a fight or something? No.
But you're mad at him.
Stop that! It's complicated.
Why? Do you need help using your phone?
No, I don't need help using my-BABY TINA Ok, what's the problem, what's the issue? Talk to me.

There's no point, ok? I call him, I invite him, he never shows up. He's always got a work meeting or a business trip or a conference call. All he cares about is work. After a while, you just stop trying.
He's still your brother. You were partners.
Yeah. I guess sometimes you just... You grow up, and grow apart. This is clearly very emotional for Tim.
Baby Tina nods her head sympathetically, then-BABY TINA Daddy, don't say no. What if
She HOLDS OUT HIS PHONE.
TINA'S NURSERY - LATER
Tim tucks Tina back into her crib next to her Story Bear and a toy horse.
This is really disappointing, and now I'm getting mad!
It's late. We can work on this tomorrow, ok?
Tina tosses the horse out of the crib.
Oh, whoopsie, your little horsie fell off.
He sets it back next to Tina then begins to close the door.
Another day won't hurt, right? Goodnight, sweetie.
As soon as the door is shut, Tina sits up and presses a rewind button on the chest of Story Bear--
Goodnight sweetie. (tape rewinding) Horsie fell off-(tape rewinding) Tabitha?!
Tina smiles.
SQ700 - FAMILY REUNION TEMPLETON HOUSE - THE NEXT MORNING

Tim brushes his teeth when the house begins to shake. Downstairs, Tabitha looks up from her homework. Baby Tina's eyes light up. Tim looks out the window and watches a helicopter touch down in the street in front of the house. A mysterious bundled up figure exits the helicopter and makes his way to the front door. DING DONG! CLOSE ON-- a finger presses the DOORBELL once more. Back inside-TIM
Tim opens the door to reveal-HIS BROTHER Tim is surprised, not altogether unpleasantly.
Where is she?! Where is she?!
He pushes past Tim and looks around.
Where's who? What?
I came as fast as I could. Ted?!
I cancelled all my meetings.
What are you doing here? Why are you yelling?
Where's Tabitha? Tabitha?!
Where is she, Tim?!
Carol, carrying Tina, and Tabitha enter from the other room. Ted!
Tabitha, thrilled, runs up to him-TABITHA Uncle Ted! You're here, you're
He kneels down and starts examining her.
Which arm is it? Left or right? Huh?
Is it your fibula? Talk to me. Whoa.
Good dilation. Open up, say ahhhh.
What are you doing?
Are you ok? You look ok.
Don't worry, I'm fine Uncle Ted.
(relieved) That's my girl.
He hands her a wad of cash.
What brings you into town?
Well, Tabitha fell off her pony. She did? She did? I did?
CAROL TABITHA
Tim left me a voice mail. He did? I did? You did.
TED
Ted holds up his phone and plays the ransom note style
Hello. This is your brother. Help? Tabitha fell off horsie. Good night, sweetie.
Well, that was unsettling.
Precious, watching this through the window, kicks over a garden gnome in outrage. Everyone turns and looks at Tim for an explanation.
I swear I didn't leave that message!
Baby Tina smiles and makes an 'oops' face. Tim realizes she did it and has to quickly cover-TIM (CONT'D) On purpose. I...
Butt-dialed?
With my butt. While I was talking in my sleep, I do that sometimes.
You sleep-butt-dialed me?
With my butt.
I knew there must be a perfectly... logical explanation.
The brothers stare at each other.
And there it is! All that matters is that you're here!
Woohoo, yay!
Absolutely. For now.
Baby Tina gives Tim a secret 'thumbs-up' and reaches for Uncle Ted. Tim watches off to the side alone as his family surrounds his brother like a returning hero-TABITHA
Sorry sweetie, I can't do Christmas on the twenty-fifth.
But Uncle Ted, I missed you so much!

Then all sound fades and things seems to move in slow motion as Tim watches Tabitha-- GIVE HER UNCLE A KISS ON THE CHEEK. It's like Tim is in a dream-- or a nightmare. Finally Tim SNAPS OUT OF IT to focus on his brother-TIM Can I talk to you in the kitchen? Alone?
IN THE KITCHEN - MOMENTS LATER
Ted and Tim talk in hushed tones as Tina watches from her high chair. Ted looks doubtfully at Tina.
The baby, Tim?
Shhhh! She's been sent from... up there...
Up... stairs?
That's right! BabyCorp!
Tim and Baby Tina look up meaningfully. Ted looks up too, but keeps one eye on Tim.
(leans in) So you're saying the voice mail was some kind of call for help? Yes!
(then) No! She comes from a secret corporation run by babies!
Tim, I'm going to give you the name of a doctor, Roy Federman, just tell him I sent you, okay?
IN THE OTHER ROOM
Tabitha literally has her ear pressed against a cup on the door.
What are they saying?
We should give them some privacy.
Here you go.
Tabitha hands Carol a cup of her own, and the two continue to listen.
Oh, thanks!
(through the door) I'm not crazy! BACK IN THE KITCHEN
Baby Tina is getting fed up as Tim and Ted ignore her and lock eyes with each other-TIM

Baby Tina opens her mouth to talk but she's interrupted-TED She can't talk, Tim. She tries to correct him but she's cut off-TIM Stop interrupting! Baby Tina keeps trying to talk but can't get a word in. Tim and Ted are too caught up to notice.
Interrupting what? I don't hear anything.
Except your obnoxious voice, you hear that.
I have a beautiful voice.
Fine! I can prove that everything I'm saying is real. Oh yeah? Yeah!
TIM
Tim holds up a PACIFIER.
Suck it, Ted.
I beg your pardon? You, suck.
No, no, Tim. You suck.
IN THE OTHER ROOM
Carol covers Tabitha's ears.
Time to go buy a Christmas tree!
Carol rushes Tabitha outside.
But we already have a Christmas tree.
No we don't! Yes we do!
No we don't. Yes we do.
BACK IN THE KITCHEN
Suck it, suck it, suck it, suck it, suck it.
Get that pacie out of my face!
Exasperated, Baby Tina opens her briefcase-BABY TINA
Tim and Ted look up, thrown.
Why don't you both suck it?
-- and JAMS PACIFIERS INTO THEIR MOUTHS.
(pacifier in his mouth) She can talk!
(pacifier in his mouth) A lot!
She rips off her onesie to reveal the suit underneath and sucks a pacifier of her own.
(pacifier in her mouth) Buckle up boys!
TIM AND TED HURTLING THROUGH SPACE AND TIME
Wooo!!! Baby on board!

As they approach BabyCorp headquarters-TIM That's what I'm talking about! TED IS FLOODED WITH MEMORIES OF HIS TIME AT THE COMPANY It's like a corporate Ludovico treatment... until WHAM-THEY LAND IN THE PLAZA Boss Baby and Baby Tina land dramatically. Tim collapses to the floor. Wow...
I'm home...
Yeah, see? It's all real!
They stop in front of a GIANT STATUE OF BOSS BABY.
Whoa. Is that... me?
If you're fishing for a compliment... you caught one! You are a legend in this joint! A statue?!
Well I did single-handedly save the company.
Double-handedly, right? We were partners.
Really? I don't see your statue.
I still love you daddy!
He continues to the elevator.
They got the head size right...
ELEVATOR - CONTINUOUS
Crammed inside, the three rise up over all of BabyCorp.
Going upsies!
Take it from me, you have to be aggressive if you want to get ahead. Climb that corporate ladder until you're the last baby standing at the top.
Actually, I prioritize a good worklife balance and a positive environment where my ideas are valued.
Ted laughs a DEEP, HEARTY LAUGH. Then-TED Oh, you're serious?
You're funny, Uncle Ted! (then) I wonder why they didn't mention that in your file?
DING! Tina steps out as they reach the TIPPIEST TOP floor. Top floor!
(worried) My file? You saw my file? Yep.
BABY TINA
What does it say?!
It says a lot.
THE SITUATION ROOM

It's like an air traffic control tower overlooking heaven. The middle of the room is dominated by a HUGE GLOBE INSET INTO THE FLOOR. Babies use sticks to push toy models around from one coordinate to another.
This is the crisis center. This is where we monitor all threats to babies around the world. And Hawaii.
Tim goofs around on the globe. Half in the globe, Tim pops up in Japan.
This is so cool, this is so cool! Rrrrr! Godzilllllla! Ah run for your life, run for your life!
Why did we bring him?
Daddy, get down from there. We can play in it later when no one is looking.
Tina leads Ted away from the globe as Tim pops out of the ocean. He jumps out of the globe and follows Tina and Ted to a wall of monitors.
(to Tina) What's the crisis?
Yeah! Here we go. What's the crisis, sweetie?
(calling out to a baby) Rusty, roll it! (to Tim and Ted) He's new.
One of the screens shows a PROMOTIONAL VIDEO FOR THE CENTER. Dr. Armstrong speaks over footage of babies performing different jobs.
Just imagine-- baby surgeons, uh oh! Or baby astronauts. Floaty, floaty, float!
The earth rotates to reveal Dr. Armstrong's face on the other side of the planet.
Yes, babies are going to run the world one day...
The real Dr. Armstrong parts a curtain and steps forward
pipe, often wearing tweed-DR. ARMSTRONG (CONT'D)
Armstrong catches himself and dials down the menacing edge.
Hello, my name is Dr. Erwin Armstrong, founder of The Acorn Center for Advanced Childhood.
Wait a minute, that's Tabitha's school!
Armstrong stands in a classroom between two students: NATHAN and MEGHAN.
Here at the Center, we believe babies are the ultimate learning machines. Isn't that right, little
Yeah! Ok!
DR. ARMSTRONG
Armstrong materializes in front of a giant clock.
They should use this precious time to develop faster, push harder. Parents? They have the best intentions, of course, but they're not experts. (MORE)
After all, the only thing holding your child back is... you.
A beat as Armstrong stares out at the viewer. You! Tim is aghast.
(sotto) This explains why Tabitha's been pulling away from me. It's the school!
Everybody wave! Bye mommy, bye daddy! Sayonara! Bye mommy, bye daddy!
The video ends.
Now, in the past six months, these schools have been popping up all
over the world.
So what's the problem?
The school is the problem.
Yeah, school is evil!
No, Daddy, not all schools are evil. Just this one. Ok? Okay.
If Armstrong's philosophy keeps spreading, it could be the end of childhood. No!
Childhood was the worst three years of my life.
(sad) That's so sad... You probably just didn't do it right. (brightening) But luckily, you get a do-over! A second chance!
She pulls them through the floor and into a stark white 'clean' room where baby scientists in white hazmat suits and welding masks pour GLOWING BABY FORMULA into a crucible.
BabyCorp has developed a new superduper baby formula that can turn a grown-up back into a baby. We have it in cherry, grape, orange, and
For real?
Wait, wait, wait, wait, wait. You want me to be a baby?!
Hey, it's what you do best!
So you can infiltrate the school and find out what Dr. Armstrong is really up to. BabyCorp will take care of the rest!
Tina pulls out the pacies. SQ900 - DOWNSIZING
TIM'S KITCHEN
Baby Tina opens her briefcase and very carefully pulls out a BOTTLE OF FORMULA.
It's the perfect disguise! And it only lasts 48 teensy weensy hours.
Oh, I am in on the mission!
Tina slaps Tim's hand away from the bottle.
Sorry Pops! It's not your mission...
(reading the bottle) Warning: may cause drooling, babbling, emotional outbursts, fits of laughter, loss of bowel control... chubby thighs? I think some guys at my gym take this stuff.
Ted squirts a drop of the formula onto his finger and his ENTIRE HAND SHRINKS.

Ah! My texting hand! BABY TINA (to Tim) Even though it is your daughter's school... Doesn't seem fair, does it? Jumping at the chance to help his daughter-- and one-up his brother-- Tim takes the bottle of formula.
(deadpan) Daddy, no, please stop.
Tim TAKES A SIP. He reverts back to a TEENAGER.
What! It actually works! It's mine!
Ted sticks his tiny fingers up Tim's nose and wrestles the bottle from Tim. He TAKES A SIP. He also reverts to a TEENAGER. Tina's reverse psychology is working.
I'm better at this kind of thing!
